channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Fully Funded University of Cambridge PhD Studentship 2027 in Vaccine Policy and Health Economics (UK)
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Applications Open for a Fully Funded PhD at the University of Cambridge The University of Cambridge is inviting applications for a prestigious fully funded PhD studentship focused on evaluating the United Kingdom’s vaccine programme through a holistic decision-making framework. This exciting three-year doctoral research opportunity is based within the Department of Veterinary Medicine and forms […]

The post Fully Funded University of Cambridge PhD Studentship 2027 in Vaccine Policy and Health Economics (UK) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for a Fully Funded PhD at the University of Cambridge

The University of Cambridge is inviting applications for a prestigious fully funded PhD studentship focused on evaluating the United Kingdom’s vaccine programme through a holistic decision-making framework. This exciting three-year doctoral research opportunity is based within the Department of Veterinary Medicine and forms part of the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) Health Protection Research Unit (HPRU) in Vaccines and Immunisation.

The project offers an exceptional opportunity for aspiring researchers interested in public health, infectious disease epidemiology, vaccine policy, health economics, and mathematical modelling. Successful candidates will work alongside leading experts from some of the United Kingdom’s most respected public health and academic institutions.

About the Research Project

The PhD project is embedded within the NIHR Health Protection Research Unit in Vaccines and Immunisation, a major collaboration involving:

  • The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA)
  • The London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM)
  • University College London (UCL)
  • The University of Cambridge

The successful student will be based at the University of Cambridge under the supervision of Professor Caroline Trotter and will collaborate closely with researchers and public health experts across the HPRU partnership.

Research Focus

Vaccination programmes are often evaluated based on individual vaccines or specific diseases. However, vaccine decision-making can have broader implications for an entire national immunisation programme.

This PhD seeks to develop a comprehensive framework that enables policymakers to assess vaccine decisions more holistically by considering impacts across the wider immunisation system.

The research will involve:

  1. Developing a decision-making framework for vaccine policy.
  2. Applying the framework to real-world vaccine case studies.
  3. Assessing how policy decisions may change when broader programme impacts are considered.
  4. Refining the framework based on evidence and practical application.
  5. Contributing new insights to vaccine policy and public health decision-making.

The project aims to strengthen the evidence base for future vaccination strategies while helping policymakers make more informed and effective decisions.

Skills and Experience You Will Gain

The PhD provides extensive interdisciplinary training and professional development opportunities.

Successful candidates will develop expertise in:

Infectious Disease Epidemiology

  • Understanding disease transmission dynamics
  • Evaluating vaccination programme impacts
  • Population-level health analysis

Mathematical Modelling

  • Modelling vaccine-preventable diseases
  • Predicting health outcomes
  • Assessing intervention effectiveness

Health Economics

  • Economic evaluation of vaccination programmes
  • Cost-effectiveness analysis
  • Health policy decision-making

Public Engagement and Involvement

A unique aspect of this project is its emphasis on public participation.

The successful candidate will:

  • Design a public involvement strategy
  • Engage communities in vaccine research discussions
  • Incorporate public perspectives into vaccine policy research
  • Develop communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Funding and Financial Support

This studentship provides substantial financial support for the duration of the PhD.

Funding includes:

  • Full tuition fees at the UK Home Fee Rate
  • Tax-free maintenance stipend starting at £20,199 per year
  • Funding for three years
  • Research costs covered through the NIHR Health Protection Research Unit in Immunisation

This financial package allows students to focus on high-impact research while receiving comprehensive academic and professional support.

Why Apply?

This opportunity offers several unique advantages:

Study at a World-Leading University

The University of Cambridge is consistently ranked among the world’s top universities and is internationally recognized for excellence in research and innovation.

Work with Leading Experts

Students will collaborate with experts from:

  • University of Cambridge
  • UK Health Security Agency
  • London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine
  • University College London

Influence Future Vaccine Policy

Research findings could contribute directly to the development of vaccination policies and public health strategies in the UK and beyond.

Build High-Demand Skills

Graduates will gain expertise highly valued in:

  • Public health agencies
  • Government institutions
  • International health organizations
  • Academic research institutions
  • Healthcare policy organizations

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should:

  • Hold or expect to obtain at least a UK 2:1 Honours Degree or equivalent
  • Have an academic background relevant to the project
  • Demonstrate strong research potential
  • Show interest in vaccine policy, epidemiology, modelling, or health economics
  • Be willing to engage with interdisciplinary teams and public stakeholders

Application Process

Interested candidates are encouraged to contact the project supervisor before submitting a formal application.

Required Steps

  1. Contact Professor Caroline Trotter to discuss the project.
  2. Review the University of Cambridge postgraduate application guidance.
  3. Submit a formal application through the University of Cambridge system.
  4. Include reference number PP49859 in all correspondence and applications.

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 10 July 2026
  • Interview Period: Before the end of July 2026
  • PhD Start Date: January 2027

Commitment to Equality and Inclusion

The University of Cambridge actively promotes equality, diversity, and inclusion and welcomes applications from all sections of society.

How to Apply

Applicants should contact the project supervisor before applying and review the detailed application guidance provided by the Department of Veterinary Medicine.

For additional information, visit the University of Cambridge Department of Veterinary Medicine postgraduate admissions page and prepare your application before the deadline of 10 July 2026.

This is an outstanding opportunity for aspiring researchers seeking to contribute to the future of vaccine policy, public health decision-making, and health protection research while studying at one of the world’s most prestigious universities.

Apply Here 

For more information on this scholarship visit here 

For more scholarship opportunities on OFY, click here

The post Fully Funded University of Cambridge PhD Studentship 2027 in Vaccine Policy and Health Economics (UK) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: U.S. Department of State Launches Up to $60 Million Funding Opportunity to Strengthen Uganda’s Health System Through the Health Foreign Assistance MOU Implementation Plan
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The U.S. Department of State, through its Bureau of Global Health Security and Diplomacy (GHSD), has announced a major funding opportunity under the Advancing Global Health Annual Program Statement (APS) aimed at supporting the implementation of the Health Foreign Assistance Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the United States Government and the Government of Uganda. With […]

The post U.S. Department of State Launches Up to $60 Million Funding Opportunity to Strengthen Uganda’s Health System Through the Health Foreign Assistance MOU Implementation Plan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Department of State, through its Bureau of Global Health Security and Diplomacy (GHSD), has announced a major funding opportunity under the Advancing Global Health Annual Program Statement (APS) aimed at supporting the implementation of the Health Foreign Assistance Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the United States Government and the Government of Uganda.

With up to $60 million in available funding and up to 15 anticipated awards, this initiative seeks to accelerate Uganda’s transition toward a sustainable, accountable, and government-led health system capable of maintaining and expanding gains in public health outcomes.

Organizations with the expertise, capacity, and commitment to strengthening health systems are invited to submit Statements of Interest (SOIs) for projects that align with the priorities outlined in the Uganda MOU Implementation Plan.

Funding Overview

Funding Opportunity Details

  • Funding Opportunity Title: Advancing Global Health
  • Funding Opportunity Number: DFOP0017890
  • Addendum Title: Health Foreign Assistance Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) Implementation in Uganda
  • Administering Agency: U.S. Department of State, Bureau of Global Health Security and Diplomacy (GHSD)
  • Total Funding Available: Up to $60,000,000
  • Number of Awards: Up to 15
  • Project Duration: Up to 5 years
  • Application Process: Two-step process
  • First Step: Submission of a Statement of Interest (SOI)

Important Deadlines

  • Deadline for Questions: June 17, 2026
  • Statement of Interest (SOI) Deadline: July 31, 2026, at 5:00 PM EDT (GMT-4)

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all program guidance and amendment documents before submitting questions or applications.

Why This Funding Opportunity Matters

Uganda is currently implementing a transformative five-year health transition framework covering the period from April 2026 through December 2030.

The framework is designed to:

  • Strengthen national health systems
  • Improve disease prevention and epidemic control
  • Enhance healthcare service delivery
  • Increase accountability and transparency
  • Expand government ownership of health programs
  • Ensure long-term sustainability of health investments

The initiative supports Uganda’s efforts to maintain progress in:

  • HIV prevention and treatment
  • Tuberculosis control
  • Malaria prevention and response
  • Maternal, newborn, and child health (MNCH)
  • Immunization services
  • Outbreak preparedness and response
  • Health security systems

A key feature of the MOU is the gradual transition from donor-driven implementation models toward government-led and performance-based programming, enabling Uganda to take greater ownership of its healthcare future.

Key Areas of Investment

The Department of State is seeking innovative and capable partners to support two major implementation tracks.

Track 1: Sustainable Faith- and Community-Based Health Systems

Faith-based and community-based health facilities play a critical role in Uganda’s healthcare landscape.

These institutions contribute significantly to:

  • HIV treatment services
  • Tuberculosis case detection
  • Malaria treatment
  • Child immunization programs
  • Maternal healthcare
  • Outbreak detection and response

However, many remain dependent on externally funded projects.

This funding opportunity aims to help these institutions become financially sustainable and operationally resilient.

Objective 1: Establish and Scale Sustainable Community-Based Health Financing Mechanisms

Organizations may propose initiatives that create sustainable financing models capable of reducing dependence on donor funding.

Potential activities include:

Community Health Insurance Development

  • Establishing new community health insurance schemes
  • Strengthening governance and oversight systems
  • Developing digital enrollment platforms
  • Implementing claims management systems
  • Building actuarial capacity
  • Improving financial management systems

Income Generation and Revenue Diversification

Organizations are encouraged to:

  • Link community health insurance schemes with income-generating initiatives
  • Strengthen financial resilience among participating communities
  • Reduce premium payment defaults
  • Improve long-term sustainability

Equitable Access

Projects should also:

  • Conduct willingness-to-pay studies
  • Introduce sustainable user fee structures
  • Establish transparent waiver systems for vulnerable populations

Objective 2: Sustain Integrated Service Delivery in Faith- and Community-Based Facilities

Recipients will help ensure uninterrupted delivery of critical health services during the transition period.

Priority service areas include:

  • HIV treatment and prevention
  • Tuberculosis services
  • Malaria programs
  • Maternal and child health
  • Immunization
  • Global health security interventions

Expected activities include:

Integrated Care Models

  • One-stop healthcare services
  • Reduction of fragmented service delivery
  • Alignment with Ministry of Health integration strategies

Strengthening Health Facility Systems

Support may include:

  • Electronic medical records
  • Laboratory networks
  • Diagnostic systems
  • Commodity management
  • Human resource management systems

Workforce Development

Organizations may support:

  • Clinical mentorship programs
  • Continuous quality improvement initiatives
  • Performance monitoring systems
  • Skills enhancement and professional development

Objective 3: Build Faith- and Community-Based Organization Capacity

Applicants may focus on strengthening institutional capacity across health facility networks.

Potential interventions include:

Organizational Capacity Building

  • Governance strengthening
  • Financial management systems
  • Strategic planning
  • Institutional development

Revenue Diversification

Projects may support:

  • Dedicated health budgets
  • Financial sustainability plans
  • Leveraging affiliated financial institutions
  • Community mobilization initiatives

Transition Planning

Organizations will be expected to develop plans that gradually shift operational costs toward domestic funding sources.

Track 2: Enabling Systems for Accountable, Government-Led Programming

This track focuses on strengthening the systems required to support Uganda’s transition to performance-based and government-led health programming.

Objective 4: Strengthen and Scale Digital Health Systems

Applicants may support the deployment, integration, and transfer of national digital health systems to government ownership.

Systems include:

  • Electronic Medical Records (EMRs)
  • eAfya
  • ClinicMaster
  • Laboratory Information Systems
  • ALIS
  • LabExpert
  • DHIS2-based surveillance systems
  • National Data Warehouse
  • eCHIS
  • iHRIS
  • National supply chain management systems

Projects should ensure:

  • Full interoperability
  • Government ownership
  • Sustainable operations
  • Transition of staffing and recurrent costs

Objective 5: Expand Sustainable Power Solutions for Health Facilities

Organizations may support the installation and maintenance of sustainable energy solutions for up to 700 health facilities by 2029.

Potential interventions include:

  • Solar power systems
  • Energy-efficient infrastructure
  • Backup power solutions
  • User training programs
  • Maintenance systems

All technology solutions should be supported by lifecycle cost analysis and coordinated with relevant government authorities.

Objective 6: Institutionalize Supply Chain Accountability and GS-1 Traceability

The Department seeks support for modernizing Uganda’s health supply chain.

Activities may include:

GS-1 Implementation

  • Product identification systems
  • Serial number tracking
  • End-to-end medicine traceability

Digital Integration

  • Linking traceability systems with LMIS platforms
  • Automated alerts and monitoring
  • Improved data reconciliation

Annual Supply Chain Audits

Recipients may conduct audits covering:

  1. Procurement and contract management
  2. Warehousing and inventory management
  3. Distribution and transportation
  4. Data quality and information systems

Objective 7: District-Level Risk Assessments and Performance Verification

Applicants may support:

Government Readiness Assessments

  • Financial management evaluations
  • Funds-flow analyses
  • IFMIS readiness assessments
  • Internal controls verification

Risk Mitigation Planning

Organizations will help local authorities:

  • Identify risks
  • Develop mitigation measures
  • Strengthen governance systems

Independent Verification

Recipients may conduct:

  • Quarterly reviews
  • Semi-annual reviews
  • Annual verification exercises
  • Performance-based payment verification

Objective 8: Indoor Residual Spraying (IRS) Quality Assurance

Projects may focus on malaria prevention through quality assurance support for Indoor Residual Spraying programs across 35 districts.

Key responsibilities may include:

  • Spray planning support
  • Household coverage verification
  • Environmental compliance monitoring
  • Safety assurance
  • Efficacy testing and verification

The goal is to verify:

  • At least 85% household spray coverage
  • 100% spray efficacy in sampled households after 30 days

Guiding Principles for Applicants

All proposed projects should align with the Department of State’s guiding principles, including:

Sustainability and Country Ownership

Projects should:

  • Strengthen Ugandan institutions
  • Promote domestic financing
  • Reduce long-term dependence on external support

Protection of Essential Services

Programs must safeguard:

  • HIV services
  • Tuberculosis services
  • Malaria interventions
  • Maternal and child health programs
  • Immunization programs

Data-Driven Accountability

Projects should:

  • Generate quality data
  • Improve decision-making
  • Strengthen accountability systems

Integrated Service Delivery

Applicants should prioritize:

  • Coordination
  • Efficiency
  • Reduced duplication
  • People-centered approaches

Local Partnerships

Organizations should collaborate with:

  • Government institutions
  • Local NGOs
  • Community-based organizations
  • Faith-based organizations
  • Private sector partners

Evidence-Based Programming

Activities must be informed by:

  • Local evidence
  • National priorities
  • Context-specific realities

Application Process

The funding opportunity uses a two-stage application process.

Step 1: Submit a Statement of Interest (SOI)

Organizations must submit a concise SOI outlining:

  • Proposed objectives
  • Project approach
  • Expected outcomes
  • Organizational capacity

Step 2: Invitation for Full Proposal

Selected applicants may be invited to submit a full proposal for further consideration.

Interested organizations may submit SOIs addressing one or multiple objectives.

Apply Here 

Final Thoughts

This significant investment represents one of the most ambitious efforts to strengthen Uganda’s health systems while advancing long-term country ownership and sustainability. Organizations with expertise in health systems strengthening, digital health, supply chain management, health financing, governance, community health, and public health service delivery have a unique opportunity to contribute to Uganda’s transition toward a resilient, accountable, and government-led health system.

With up to $60 million in funding available, successful applicants will play a critical role in supporting healthcare transformation, improving service delivery, strengthening epidemic preparedness, and ensuring that health gains are sustained for generations to come.

For more information on this grant opportunity visit here

For more grant opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post U.S. Department of State Launches Up to $60 Million Funding Opportunity to Strengthen Uganda’s Health System Through the Health Foreign Assistance MOU Implementation Plan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Institute for Future Conflict Nonresident Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Scholars, and Strategic Thinkers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Institute for Future Conflict (IFC) at the United States Air Force Academy (USAFA) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Nonresident Research Fellowship Program for the 2026–2027 academic year. This prestigious fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity for researchers, academics, military professionals, policy experts, and strategic thinkers interested in the future of warfare, security, […]

The post Institute for Future Conflict Nonresident Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Scholars, and Strategic Thinkers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Institute for Future Conflict (IFC) at the United States Air Force Academy (USAFA) has officially opened applications for its highly competitive Nonresident Research Fellowship Program for the 2026–2027 academic year. This prestigious fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity for researchers, academics, military professionals, policy experts, and strategic thinkers interested in the future of warfare, security, technology, and strategic competition.

The fellowship is designed to bring together emerging and established experts whose work contributes to understanding how future conflicts may evolve and how nations can better prepare for rapidly changing security environments. Through research, collaboration, professional engagement, and thought leadership, fellows will play an important role in advancing discussions on future warfare and statecraft.

Applications are currently open and will close on June 30, 2026.

About the Institute for Future Conflict

The Institute for Future Conflict (IFC) is based at the United States Air Force Academy and exists to advance understanding and preparation for future conflict through research, education, leadership development, and external engagement.

The institute focuses on helping develop adaptive military leaders capable of responding effectively to evolving global security challenges. Its work examines a wide range of topics, including:

  • Emerging technologies and warfare
  • Strategic competition among nations
  • Adversary doctrine and military innovation
  • Force design and military modernization
  • Operational adaptation
  • Defense policy and strategy
  • The relationship between military power and society
  • Future operational environments
  • National security challenges

Through its partnerships with government agencies, academic institutions, military organizations, and industry leaders, the institute seeks to develop innovative approaches to future security challenges.

About the Nonresident Research Fellowship

The Nonresident Research Fellowship is an annual program that runs alongside the academic year and lasts for one calendar year.

The fellowship brings together individuals from diverse backgrounds who share a common interest in understanding and shaping the future of conflict and strategic competition.

Rather than following a rigid structure, the fellowship is designed to be flexible and tailored to each fellow’s expertise, research interests, and professional goals.

Successful applicants will work closely with the Fellowship Director to create individualized engagement plans that maximize their contributions while supporting their own professional development.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive several valuable benefits throughout the fellowship period.

$2,000 Honorarium

Each fellow receives a $2,000 honorarium for active participation in the program and contributions to the intellectual life of the Institute for Future Conflict.

Travel Support

The institute covers travel expenses associated with required fellowship events and workshops.

Access to High-Level Networks

Fellows gain access to a unique network of:

  • Military leaders
  • Government officials
  • Policy professionals
  • Academic researchers
  • Defense experts
  • Industry innovators
  • Former and current fellows

Research and Publication Opportunities

Participants have opportunities to:

  • Publish original research
  • Develop policy analysis papers
  • Produce strategic commentary
  • Contribute to ongoing debates on national security and future warfare

Professional Development

The fellowship provides valuable opportunities to:

  • Refine research agendas
  • Expand professional networks
  • Engage with defense audiences
  • Develop publication pipelines
  • Collaborate with leading experts

Fellowship Activities

The fellowship offers flexibility in how participants contribute.

Examples of fellowship activities include:

Research and Writing

Fellows may:

  • Conduct original research
  • Produce policy papers
  • Write strategic analyses
  • Publish commentary on future conflict topics

Collaborative Projects

Participants can collaborate with IFC-affiliated scholars on:

  • Research projects
  • Publications
  • Applied defense initiatives
  • Strategic studies

Presentations and Public Engagement

Fellows may:

  • Deliver lectures and presentations
  • Engage military audiences
  • Brief policymakers
  • Speak to industry stakeholders
  • Participate in public discussions

Cadet Mentorship

Participants can contribute to educational initiatives by:

  • Supporting cadet research projects
  • Advising student researchers
  • Participating in Future Conflict Minor activities

Workshops and Wargames

Fellows are encouraged to engage in:

  • Strategic workshops
  • Wargaming exercises
  • Speaker series
  • Research forums

Required Fellowship Deliverable

Each fellow must produce at least one written product for publication on the Institute for Future Conflict website.

This publication may include:

  • Research articles
  • Policy analysis
  • Strategic commentary
  • Defense-focused essays
  • Future warfare assessments

Fellowship Timeline

August 21, 2026

Selected fellows will attend a kickoff workshop at the United States Air Force Academy.

This workshop serves as an opportunity to:

  • Present project ideas
  • Receive feedback from peers and experts
  • Establish research goals
  • Set publication deadlines

Summer 2027

Fellows will reconvene to present their completed research and share insights gained during the fellowship year.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship welcomes applicants from a variety of professional backgrounds.

Early-Career Scholars

Doctoral candidates and emerging researchers can use the fellowship to:

  • Build academic networks
  • Strengthen research portfolios
  • Develop publication opportunities

Mid-Career and Senior Scholars

Experienced researchers may use the fellowship to:

  • Engage defense audiences
  • Influence policy discussions
  • Expand professional collaborations

Practitioners and Professionals

Military personnel, government officials, analysts, and industry professionals can leverage the fellowship to:

  • Connect practical experience with research
  • Contribute operational insights
  • Shape future defense thinking

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the fellowship, applicants must:

Citizenship Requirement

  • Be a United States citizen

Education Requirement

Applicants must possess:

  • A master’s degree or higher; OR
  • Equivalent professional experience

Research Focus Requirement

Applicants must demonstrate a strong focus on:

  • Future conflict
  • Strategic competition
  • National security
  • Defense studies
  • Military innovation
  • Related fields

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following materials:

1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

A comprehensive CV outlining educational background, research experience, publications, and professional accomplishments.

2. Cover Letter

A one- to two-page cover letter explaining:

  • Professional background
  • Alignment with the Institute for Future Conflict
  • Proposed fellowship project
  • Expected contributions to the program

3. Writing Sample

Applicants must provide a previously published writing sample demonstrating their research, analytical, and communication abilities.

The Institute emphasizes originality and expects all submitted materials to accurately reflect the applicant’s own work.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted via email.

Send application materials to:

Dr. Kerry Chávez
kerry.chavez@afacademy.af.edu

CC:

Dr. Layne Hancock
cary.hancock@afacademy.af.edu

Apply Here

Application Deadline

June 30, 2026

Applicants should ensure that all required documents are submitted before the close of business on the deadline date.

Why This Fellowship Matters

As emerging technologies, geopolitical competition, artificial intelligence, cyber warfare, and evolving military doctrines continue to reshape the global security landscape, understanding future conflict has become more important than ever.

The Institute for Future Conflict Nonresident Research Fellowship offers a unique platform for experts and emerging leaders to contribute meaningful research, influence strategic thinking, and engage directly with military and policy communities shaping the future of national and international security.

For scholars, practitioners, and professionals interested in the future of warfare and strategic competition, this fellowship represents a valuable opportunity to advance both personal expertise and broader discussions on global security.

For more information on this fellowship visit here

For more fellowship opportunities on OFY click here  

The post Institute for Future Conflict Nonresident Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers, Scholars, and Strategic Thinkers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026: Fully Virtual Pan-African Policy Innovation Challenge for Young Leaders Driving Africa’s Future
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET) has officially opened applications for the ACET Youth Policy Lab (AYPL) 2026, a unique continent-wide policy innovation challenge designed to place young Africans at the center of policymaking, innovation, and economic transformation. Under the theme, “From Coordination to Commercialization: Youth, Innovation & Growth,” the 2026 edition invites ambitious […]

The post ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026: Fully Virtual Pan-African Policy Innovation Challenge for Young Leaders Driving Africa’s Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Center for Economic Transformation (ACET) has officially opened applications for the ACET Youth Policy Lab (AYPL) 2026, a unique continent-wide policy innovation challenge designed to place young Africans at the center of policymaking, innovation, and economic transformation.

Under the theme, “From Coordination to Commercialization: Youth, Innovation & Growth,” the 2026 edition invites ambitious young Africans to step into the role of policymakers and tackle some of Africa’s most pressing development challenges through evidence-based policy design, collaboration, mentorship, and leadership development.

The ACET Youth Policy Lab is much more than a competition. It is an immersive learning experience that combines the excitement of a hackathon with the rigor of real-world policy development, equipping participants with practical skills in governance, innovation, leadership, and policy analysis.

For young Africans who aspire to influence public policy, drive innovation, and contribute to sustainable development across the continent, this opportunity offers a powerful platform to learn, collaborate, and create impact.

What is the ACET Youth Policy Lab?

The ACET Youth Policy Lab is a simulation-based policy innovation platform that empowers young Africans to engage directly with policymaking processes.

Participants are placed within a fictional institution known as the National Youth Transformation Authority (NYTA), where they assume the role of decision-makers responsible for developing solutions to real-world development challenges facing African countries.

The program is specifically designed to:

  • Strengthen youth participation in policymaking
  • Develop policy analysis and problem-solving skills
  • Promote innovation-driven development solutions
  • Enhance leadership capabilities
  • Foster collaboration among young Africans
  • Increase youth representation in governance spaces

Unlike traditional competitions, participants are challenged to think critically about policy trade-offs, institutional constraints, and implementation realities.

Theme for 2026: From Coordination to Commercialization – Youth, Innovation & Growth

The 2026 Youth Policy Lab focuses on one of Africa’s most important development priorities: transforming innovative ideas into sustainable economic growth opportunities.

Participants will explore how effective policy coordination, innovation ecosystems, and commercialization strategies can create jobs, strengthen industries, and accelerate Africa’s economic transformation.

The theme encourages young leaders to design practical solutions that support inclusive growth while addressing systemic barriers that often limit innovation and entrepreneurship across the continent.

Policy Challenges Participants Will Address

Through the simulation, participants will work on policy challenges connected to:

1. Youth Employment and Skills Development

Participants will explore policies that can:

  • Reduce youth unemployment
  • Improve workforce readiness
  • Strengthen technical and vocational education
  • Enhance employability and entrepreneurship opportunities

2. Innovation and Commercialization

Teams will examine strategies for:

  • Supporting innovators and startups
  • Commercializing research and innovation
  • Building stronger innovation ecosystems
  • Scaling locally developed solutions

3. Digital Transformation

Participants will develop ideas related to:

  • Digital inclusion
  • Emerging technologies
  • Digital entrepreneurship
  • Technology-driven public services

4. Governance and Institutional Reform

The program challenges participants to consider:

  • Effective governance structures
  • Public sector reforms
  • Accountability systems
  • Institutional efficiency

5. Financing and Donor Coordination

Teams will analyze ways to:

  • Mobilize development financing
  • Improve donor coordination
  • Strengthen resource allocation
  • Increase investment effectiveness

Experience Real-World Policymaking

One of the most exciting aspects of the ACET Youth Policy Lab is its realism.

Participants will not simply generate ideas; they will navigate the same challenges policymakers face every day, including:

  • Limited national budgets
  • IMF restrictions
  • Political pressures
  • Competing stakeholder interests
  • Resource constraints
  • Implementation challenges

This approach ensures participants gain practical experience in balancing ambition with reality.

Why You Should Apply

The ACET Youth Policy Lab offers a wide range of professional and leadership benefits.

Build Practical Policy Skills

Participants gain hands-on experience in:

  • Policy analysis
  • Research
  • Strategic thinking
  • Public problem-solving
  • Evidence-based policymaking

Receive Expert Mentorship

Selected participants will receive guidance from leading African experts and practitioners.

Mentors will help teams refine ideas, strengthen proposals, and improve policy recommendations.

Collaborate Across Africa

Participants will work with talented young leaders from different countries and backgrounds.

This cross-cultural collaboration strengthens teamwork and exposes participants to diverse perspectives.

Develop Leadership Skills

The program helps participants enhance:

  • Communication skills
  • Decision-making abilities
  • Negotiation techniques
  • Leadership capacity

Create a Policy Proposal

Each team will develop a comprehensive three-page policy proposal that addresses a critical development challenge.

Pitch Your Ideas

Participants will present their solutions during a live virtual pitch event before experts and judges.

Gain Access to Future Opportunities

The program offers pathways to:

  • Internship opportunities
  • Ambassador roles
  • Professional mentorship
  • Regional visibility
  • Recognition across ACET platforms

Additional Benefits for Winning Participants

Winning participants will receive:

  • Cash vouchers
  • Internship opportunities
  • Mentorship support
  • Certificates of participation and achievement
  • Regional recognition through ACET networks and platforms

These rewards are designed to support participants’ continued growth and leadership development.

How the ACET Youth Policy Lab Works

The program follows a structured journey that combines learning, collaboration, coaching, and competition.

Step 1: Application Stage

Young Africans submit their applications and demonstrate their interest in policy innovation and leadership.

Step 2: Team Formation

Selected applicants are grouped into six teams, each consisting of five participants.

Step 3: Mentorship and Coaching

Teams participate in mentorship sessions and receive guidance from experts.

Step 4: Policy Development

Participants collaborate to design evidence-based policy solutions and prepare their proposals.

Step 5: Virtual Pitch Event

Teams present their recommendations during the final virtual pitch competition.

Step 6: Winners Announcement

Top-performing teams are recognized and rewarded.

Step 7: YES Ambassador Program

Participants may have opportunities to join a regional youth network through the YES Ambassador Program.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, candidates must meet the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must be between 18 and 35 years old.

Citizenship Requirement

Applicants must be citizens of African countries.

Language Requirement

Applicants must be fluent in either:

  • English
  • French

Commitment Requirement

Applicants must be able to dedicate approximately 4–6 hours weekly throughout the program.

Important Dates

Applications Open

15 June 2026

Application Deadline

7 July 2026

Team Shortlisting

8 July – 31 July 2026

Team Interviews

1 August – 31 August 2026

Participant Onboarding

1 September – 18 September 2026

Coaching and Mentorship Period

21 September – 16 October 2026

Grand Finale and Virtual Pitch Event

4 November 2026

Winners Announcement

Following the final pitch event.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete the Online Application Form through the official ACET Youth Policy Lab portal.

For inquiries:
youthpolicylab@acetforafrica.org

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all program information before submitting their applications.

Why This Opportunity Matters for Africa’s Future

Africa has the world’s youngest population, making youth participation in policymaking essential for sustainable development.

The ACET Youth Policy Lab recognizes that young people are not merely beneficiaries of policy decisions; they are innovators, entrepreneurs, problem-solvers, and future leaders capable of driving transformative change.

By equipping young Africans with practical policymaking experience, leadership training, and access to expert mentorship, the program contributes to building a stronger generation of leaders who can shape inclusive and prosperous futures across the continent.

Visit HERE to learn more about ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026.

Conclusion

The ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious young Africans to gain hands-on experience in policymaking, innovation, leadership, and economic transformation. Through collaboration, mentorship, policy simulation, and competitive pitching, participants will develop the skills needed to influence real-world change and contribute meaningfully to Africa’s development agenda.

For young leaders passionate about governance, innovation, entrepreneurship, public policy, and sustainable development, this program offers a unique platform to learn from experts, connect with peers across Africa, and transform ideas into impactful policy solutions. Interested candidates should submit their applications before the 7 July 2026 deadline and take the next step toward becoming part of Africa’s next generation of changemakers.

Discover more global African, Leadership and Policy Development opportunities on OFY.

The post ACET Youth Policy Lab 2026: Fully Virtual Pan-African Policy Innovation Challenge for Young Leaders Driving Africa’s Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WHO Youth Council 2026–2028: Global Call for Youth Organizations to Shape International Health Policy
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The World Health Organization (WHO) has officially launched a global call for expressions of interest for the third term of the WHO Youth Council (2026–2028). This exciting opportunity invites youth-led and youth-focused organizations from around the world to play a direct role in influencing global health discussions, policies, and strategies. As one of WHO’s flagship […]

The post WHO Youth Council 2026–2028: Global Call for Youth Organizations to Shape International Health Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Health Organization (WHO) has officially launched a global call for expressions of interest for the third term of the WHO Youth Council (2026–2028). This exciting opportunity invites youth-led and youth-focused organizations from around the world to play a direct role in influencing global health discussions, policies, and strategies.

As one of WHO’s flagship youth engagement platforms, the WHO Youth Council provides an avenue for young people and youth organizations to contribute meaningfully to international health governance. Through advocacy, collaboration, innovation, and policy engagement, selected organizations will help strengthen youth participation in shaping healthier and more resilient communities worldwide.

Organizations interested in becoming part of this influential network are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline and carefully review all eligibility requirements.

About the WHO Youth Council

The WHO Youth Council was established to strengthen youth engagement within the World Health Organization by creating a structured platform through which young people can contribute to health-related decision-making processes.

The Council serves multiple purposes, including:

  • Amplifying youth voices in global health discussions
  • Providing insights from young people on emerging health challenges
  • Supporting WHO’s health promotion and advocacy initiatives
  • Encouraging innovative youth-led solutions to public health issues
  • Strengthening collaboration between youth organizations and international health stakeholders
  • Promoting youth participation in health systems and policy development

The Council reflects WHO’s commitment to ensuring that young people are not merely beneficiaries of health policies but active contributors to the design and implementation of solutions that affect their lives and communities.

Why the WHO Youth Council Matters

Young people represent a significant proportion of the global population and are increasingly recognized as critical stakeholders in addressing public health challenges.

Through the WHO Youth Council, participating organizations can:

Influence Global Health Policies

Council members have opportunities to provide perspectives and recommendations that inform WHO strategies and initiatives.

Elevate Youth Priorities

Organizations can ensure that issues affecting young people are represented in international health conversations.

Strengthen Global Networks

Members gain access to a diverse network of youth organizations, health advocates, experts, and policymakers from around the world.

Promote Youth Leadership

The Council provides a platform for organizations to demonstrate leadership and advocate for meaningful youth participation in health governance.

Contribute Innovative Solutions

Organizations can share innovative approaches and experiences that support stronger and more inclusive health systems.

Who Is WHO Looking For?

WHO is seeking a diverse and representative group of organizations from across the globe.

Eligible organizations include:

1. Youth-Led Organizations

Organizations where young people hold leadership positions and drive decision-making processes.

2. Youth-Focused Organizations

Organizations that primarily work to support, empower, or advocate for young people.

3. Youth Chapters of Larger Organizations

Youth branches or youth wings operating within larger national or international organizations.

WHO has also indicated its interest in selecting a balanced mix of organizations from both:

  • Health-related sectors
  • Non-health sectors

This multidisciplinary approach ensures diverse perspectives and strengthens the Council’s ability to address complex public health challenges.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for membership, organizations must satisfy all requirements outlined in the revised WHO Youth Council Terms of Reference.

Key eligibility expectations include:

  • Active engagement at the international level
  • Demonstrated commitment to youth engagement and participation
  • Alignment with WHO values and objectives
  • Capacity to contribute meaningfully to Council activities
  • Compliance with WHO membership requirements

Applicants are strongly encouraged to thoroughly review the Terms of Reference before submitting their application.

WHO Civil Society Commission Requirement

An important requirement for participation in the WHO Youth Council is membership in the WHO Civil Society Commission.

WHO has announced that all Youth Council members will also be expected to participate in the WHO Civil Society Commission.

Organizations that are not already members must:

  1. Submit an application to join the WHO Civil Society Commission.
  2. Complete this step before submitting their WHO Youth Council expression of interest.

WHO has clarified that applications to the Youth Council can still be considered while the organization’s Civil Society Commission application is under review.

This requirement aims to strengthen collaboration between youth organizations and broader civil society stakeholders engaged in global health initiatives.

Application Process

Organizations wishing to apply must complete all required application components.

Step 1: Complete the WHO Youth Council Application Form

Applicants must fill out the official online application form and provide all requested information.

Step 2: Submit the Tobacco-Arms Disclosure Form

In addition to the application form, organizations must complete and submit the Tobacco-Arms Disclosure Form.

Step 3: Ensure Compliance with Eligibility Requirements

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Compliance with eligibility criteria
  • Organizational experience and track record
  • Motivation for joining the Council
  • Proposed plans and contributions
  • Diversity and representation considerations

Organizations should provide clear and compelling information demonstrating how they can contribute to the Council’s mission and objectives.

APPLY HERE

Selection Criteria

WHO will assess applications using several factors, including:

Organizational Relevance

The organization’s work and activities should align with youth engagement and meaningful participation.

Experience

Applicants should demonstrate relevant experience in advocacy, leadership, public health, youth development, community engagement, or related areas.

Motivation

Organizations should clearly explain why they wish to join the Council and how they plan to contribute.

Diversity

WHO seeks broad geographic, thematic, and organizational representation to ensure that multiple perspectives are reflected within the Council.

Important Dates

Application Deadline

30 June 2026 at 23:59 CEST

Organizations are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical challenges.

First In-Person Meeting

October 2026

Selected organizations will participate in the Council’s first in-person meeting, marking the beginning of the 2026–2028 term.

Documents Applicants Must Review

Before applying, organizations should carefully read and complete the following documents:

  1. WHO Youth Council Terms of Reference
  2. Tobacco-Arms Disclosure Form

Reviewing these materials is essential for understanding the Council’s responsibilities, expectations, and operational framework.

Benefits of Joining the WHO Youth Council

Participation in the WHO Youth Council offers several valuable advantages:

  • Opportunity to engage directly with WHO
  • Influence international health policy discussions
  • Amplify youth voices at the global level
  • Collaborate with youth organizations worldwide
  • Strengthen advocacy and leadership capacity
  • Expand international partnerships and networks
  • Contribute to global public health initiatives
  • Participate in high-level dialogues and consultations
  • Increase organizational visibility within the global health ecosystem
  • Support meaningful youth participation in health systems strengthening

How to Prepare a Strong Application

Organizations seeking selection should consider the following strategies:

Demonstrate Impact

Highlight measurable achievements and successful initiatives led by your organization.

Showcase Youth Leadership

Clearly explain how young people participate in leadership and decision-making processes.

Emphasize Global Engagement

Illustrate international partnerships, collaborations, or activities that demonstrate global reach.

Present Clear Goals

Explain how your organization intends to contribute to the Council’s work during the 2026–2028 term.

Align with WHO Priorities

Demonstrate a commitment to health equity, inclusion, youth participation, and sustainable development.

Interested organizations are required to complete the online WHO Youth Council Application Form.

Click HERE to learn more about the WHO Youth Council 2026–2028.

Conclusion

The WHO Youth Council 2026–2028 presents an exceptional opportunity for youth-led and youth-focused organizations to engage directly with one of the world’s most influential health institutions. Through this platform, organizations can help shape health policies, advocate for youth priorities, contribute innovative ideas, and strengthen youth participation in global health governance.

Organizations from around the world that are passionate about advancing youth engagement and improving public health outcomes should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the 30 June 2026 deadline. Participation in the WHO Youth Council offers not only global visibility and networking opportunities but also a meaningful chance to influence the future of international health policy and practice.

Discover more global Fellowship, Health and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post WHO Youth Council 2026–2028: Global Call for Youth Organizations to Shape International Health Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PhD Scholarship Opportunity: Future Rural Africa Project (Subproject B03 “Violent Futures”) in Tanzania & Kenya – Fully Funded Ethnographic Research Position
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Future Rural Africa Project is inviting applications for a highly competitive PhD scholarship under its Subproject B03 titled “Violent Futures.” This research-focused doctoral opportunity is designed for scholars interested in understanding how emerging carbon credit initiatives influence social relations, conflict dynamics, and future-making processes in rural African contexts, particularly in Tanzania and Kenya. This […]

The post PhD Scholarship Opportunity: Future Rural Africa Project (Subproject B03 “Violent Futures”) in Tanzania & Kenya – Fully Funded Ethnographic Research Position appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Future Rural Africa Project is inviting applications for a highly competitive PhD scholarship under its Subproject B03 titled “Violent Futures.” This research-focused doctoral opportunity is designed for scholars interested in understanding how emerging carbon credit initiatives influence social relations, conflict dynamics, and future-making processes in rural African contexts, particularly in Tanzania and Kenya.

This is a long-term, field-intensive academic opportunity ideal for candidates with strong interests in anthropology, political ecology, development studies, and conflict research.

The project places special emphasis on ethnographic fieldwork, meaning selected candidates will engage in deep, immersive research within communities affected by carbon credit and climate-related development initiatives.

About the Research Project: “Violent Futures”

The Subproject B03 “Violent Futures” investigates the complex relationship between future-oriented carbon credit schemes and their real-world impacts on social structures, land use, and conflict formation in East Africa.

Key Research Focus Areas

The PhD candidate will contribute to understanding:

  1. Carbon Credit Projects and Social Transformation
    • How carbon credit and climate mitigation initiatives reshape rural economies
    • The role of global climate markets in local governance systems
  2. Conflict and Social Relations
    • Emerging tensions between communities, institutions, and investors
    • Land access disputes linked to environmental and conservation policies
  3. Ethnographic Fieldwork in Tanzania and Kenya
    • Long-term immersion in rural communities
    • Qualitative data collection including interviews, participant observation, and field notes
  4. Future-Making and Environmental Governance
    • How communities imagine and negotiate their futures under climate interventions
    • Power relations between local actors and international climate finance structures

Scholarship Details

This PhD scholarship offers a structured research environment with financial support for the full duration of the program.

Key Facts

  • Duration: 41 months
  • Monthly stipend: €1,475
  • Start date: Ideally 1 August 2026
  • Application deadline: 21 June 2026

What the Opportunity Offers

This PhD position provides a unique combination of academic training, field experience, and financial support. Key benefits include:

  • Long-term funded doctoral research
  • Access to a major interdisciplinary research project
  • Opportunity to conduct fieldwork in Tanzania and Kenya
  • Engagement with global debates on climate justice and carbon markets
  • Development of advanced ethnographic and qualitative research skills
  • Collaboration with international researchers and institutions

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

21 June 2026

Late submissions will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to prepare their documents early.

Ideal Candidate Profile

This opportunity is best suited for candidates who demonstrate:

  • Strong academic background in social sciences or related disciplines
  • Interest in anthropology, political ecology, environmental studies, or development studies
  • Experience or strong willingness to conduct ethnographic fieldwork
  • Analytical interest in climate change, governance, and conflict studies
  • Ability to work independently in field environments in East Africa
  • Strong writing and research skills at postgraduate level

Application Link

Click here for more and to apply

 

For more global scholarship opportunities, Click here

 

The post PhD Scholarship Opportunity: Future Rural Africa Project (Subproject B03 “Violent Futures”) in Tanzania & Kenya – Fully Funded Ethnographic Research Position appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program 2027: Fully Funded Nursing Doctorate in the USA for Future Nurse Scientists, Educators, Researchers, and Policy Leaders
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Boston College Connell School of Nursing (CSON) is inviting applications for its prestigious Ph.D. Program in Nursing, a fully funded doctoral program designed to prepare the next generation of nurse scientists, educators, researchers, and healthcare policy leaders. Located at Boston College in Massachusetts, United States, the program combines advanced scientific inquiry, nursing theory, and […]

The post Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program 2027: Fully Funded Nursing Doctorate in the USA for Future Nurse Scientists, Educators, Researchers, and Policy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Boston College Connell School of Nursing (CSON) is inviting applications for its prestigious Ph.D. Program in Nursing, a fully funded doctoral program designed to prepare the next generation of nurse scientists, educators, researchers, and healthcare policy leaders. Located at Boston College in Massachusetts, United States, the program combines advanced scientific inquiry, nursing theory, and rigorous research training to equip scholars with the knowledge and expertise needed to transform healthcare systems and improve patient outcomes globally.

One of the most attractive features of the program is its comprehensive financial support package. The Connell School of Nursing fully funds tuition, fees, and health insurance for all full-time Ph.D. students during their first three years of study, making it one of the most competitive nursing doctoral opportunities available.

With a strong emphasis on research excellence, leadership development, and interdisciplinary collaboration, graduates emerge prepared to influence healthcare policy, conduct groundbreaking nursing research, educate future healthcare professionals, and lead innovation across healthcare institutions worldwide.

About the Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program

The Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program integrates science, theory, and research methodologies to prepare highly skilled nurse scientists capable of addressing complex healthcare challenges.

The program attracts students from diverse academic, clinical, and professional backgrounds who share a commitment to advancing nursing science and improving healthcare delivery.

Students work closely with distinguished faculty members whose expertise spans numerous healthcare and nursing disciplines. Throughout their doctoral journey, candidates receive mentorship, research guidance, and professional development opportunities designed to support successful academic and professional careers.

Program Highlights

Fully Funded Doctoral Education

Every full-time Ph.D. student receives:

  • Full tuition coverage
  • Mandatory fees coverage
  • Health insurance coverage
  • Financial support for the first three years of study

This funding package significantly reduces financial barriers and allows students to focus fully on their academic and research pursuits.

Duration of Study

Students generally complete the Ph.D. program within four years.

Program Start Date

The program begins each Fall semester.

Academic Structure

Most students complete:

  • 20 courses
  • 46 total academic credits

The curriculum is designed to progressively develop research expertise, analytical thinking, and advanced nursing scholarship.

Curriculum Overview

The doctoral curriculum combines foundational nursing science with advanced research methods and statistical analysis.

First-Year Core Courses

Fall Semester

  1. Evidence Appraisal and Synthesis
  2. Philosophical Inquiry for Knowledge Development in Nursing
  3. Introduction to Statistics and Data Analysis
  4. Survey of Research Methods in Social and Behavioral Science

Spring Semester

  1. Strategies for Knowledge Development
  2. Advanced Qualitative Research Methods
  3. Regression Analysis for Social and Behavioral Science

These courses establish a strong foundation in research design, statistical analysis, evidence-based practice, and theoretical development.

Research Opportunities

A major strength of the Connell School Ph.D. Program is its extensive focus on research.

Students actively participate in scholarly activities and contribute to studies addressing critical healthcare challenges such as:

  • Public health and health systems
  • Chronic disease management
  • Mental health
  • Healthcare quality and safety
  • Nursing ethics
  • Genomic healthcare disparities
  • Substance use and chronic pain management

Doctoral candidates regularly present their findings at national and international conferences and publish in respected academic journals.

Many students also receive prestigious honors and recognition, including induction into the American Academy of Nursing and selection as Jonas Center for Nursing Excellence Scholars.

Distinguished Faculty Mentorship

Students benefit from mentorship by internationally recognized nursing scholars and researchers.

Faculty members guide students through:

  • Research development
  • Dissertation planning
  • Publication strategies
  • Career preparation
  • Academic leadership development

The school’s supportive academic culture helps students build confidence and expertise throughout the doctoral journey.

Career Opportunities After Graduation

Graduates of the Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program pursue leadership positions across healthcare, academia, research institutions, and government agencies.

Recent graduates have secured positions at:

  • American Nurses Foundation
  • Baylor College of Medicine
  • Boston Children’s Hospital
  • Massachusetts General Hospital
  • MGH Institute of Health Professions
  • University of Arizona
  • University of Rhode Island
  • Simmons University
  • Hartford Hospital
  • Yale National Clinician Scholars Program
  • New Hampshire Department of Health and Human Services
  • International universities and healthcare organizations

Career pathways include:

  • Nurse Scientist
  • University Professor
  • Health Policy Expert
  • Research Director
  • Clinical Researcher
  • Healthcare Executive
  • Public Health Leader
  • Academic Administrator

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Academic Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in Nursing from a nationally accredited university
  • Completed statistics course
  • Current Registered Nurse (RN) license

International Applicants

Applicants from outside the United States must possess:

  • A Bachelor of Science in Nursing equivalent to a U.S. BSN degree
  • An active nursing license in their home country

Preferred Applicant Qualifications

The admissions committee gives preference to applicants who demonstrate:

  • Minimum GPA of 3.5
  • Strong research experience
  • Clear research interests aligned with faculty expertise
  • Publications and scholarly work
  • Excellent writing ability
  • Leadership experience or leadership potential

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

1. Resume or Curriculum Vitae

Include:

  • Academic history
  • Professional experience
  • Nursing licenses
  • Research experience
  • Publications
  • Social justice activities
  • Additional language skills

2. Goal Statement

A typed, double-spaced statement of no more than four pages outlining:

  • Educational goals
  • Research interests
  • Professional aspirations

3. Scholarly Writing Sample

Examples may include:

  • Academic papers
  • Published articles
  • Training manuals
  • Clinical case analyses
  • Research manuscripts

4. Letters of Recommendation

Three recommendation letters are required from:

  • A current or recent employer or professional colleague
  • A professor familiar with your academic potential
  • A professional reference familiar with your clinical practice

5. Academic Transcripts

Official transcripts are preferred during application and required upon admission.

6. GRE Scores

GRE scores are preferred but not mandatory.

English Language Requirements

Applicants whose native language is not English must submit evidence of English proficiency.

Accepted minimum scores include:

  • TOEFL iBT: 100
  • IELTS Academic: 7.5
  • TOEFL Paper-Based Test: 600
  • TOEFL Computer-Based Test: 250

Certain applicants from English-speaking countries may qualify for exemption.

Why Choose Boston College’s Nursing Ph.D. Program?

The Connell School of Nursing stands out because of:

  • Full financial support
  • World-class faculty mentorship
  • Strong research training
  • Leadership development opportunities
  • Excellent graduate employment outcomes
  • Global academic reputation
  • Diverse and collaborative learning environment

Students graduate prepared not only to contribute to nursing science but also to shape healthcare systems and influence policy decisions worldwide.

Application Deadline

January 15 2027

Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their application materials well in advance of the deadline.

APPLY HERE

Click HERE to learn more about the Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program 2027.

Conclusion

The Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program offers an exceptional opportunity for aspiring nurse scientists and healthcare leaders seeking rigorous doctoral training in a fully funded environment. Through advanced coursework, intensive research experiences, expert mentorship, and comprehensive financial support, the program prepares graduates to address some of the most pressing healthcare challenges facing communities around the world. For nurses passionate about research, education, policy development, and healthcare innovation, this doctoral program represents a powerful pathway toward meaningful impact and long-term professional success.

Discover more global PhD and Scholarship opportunities on OFY.

The post Boston College Connell School of Nursing Ph.D. Program 2027: Fully Funded Nursing Doctorate in the USA for Future Nurse Scientists, Educators, Researchers, and Policy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD in Gender, Peace and Security at the Swedish Defence University 2027 | Salary, Research Funding and International Applicants Welcome
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Are you passionate about gender studies, security, peacebuilding, conflict studies, military institutions, and feminist approaches to international security? The Swedish Defence University (SEDU) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD position in War Studies with a specialization in Gender, Peace and Security. This prestigious doctoral opportunity provides successful candidates with full funding, a competitive […]

The post Fully Funded PhD in Gender, Peace and Security at the Swedish Defence University 2027 | Salary, Research Funding and International Applicants Welcome appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about gender studies, security, peacebuilding, conflict studies, military institutions, and feminist approaches to international security? The Swedish Defence University (SEDU) is inviting applications for a fully funded PhD position in War Studies with a specialization in Gender, Peace and Security.

This prestigious doctoral opportunity provides successful candidates with full funding, a competitive monthly salary, research support funding, and the opportunity to conduct cutting-edge research at one of Europe’s leading institutions in defence, security, and conflict studies.

The position is based in Stockholm, Sweden, and is expected to commence in January 2027.

Application Deadline: 24 August 2026

About the Swedish Defence University

The Swedish Defence University is an internationally recognized institution specializing in:

  • Defence Studies
  • Security Studies
  • Crisis Management
  • International Security
  • War Studies
  • Political Science
  • Military Science
  • Leadership and Command Studies

The university serves as Sweden’s premier institution for military and civilian education and research relating to defence, security, conflict, and crisis management.

Through its interdisciplinary academic environment, military professionals, policymakers, researchers, and students collaborate to address some of the most pressing security challenges facing societies today.

About the Department of War Studies

The Department of War Studies is one of the university’s largest academic departments, with approximately:

  • 160 staff members
  • 1,000 students and trainees

The department conducts internationally recognized research examining:

  • War and armed conflict
  • Military organizations
  • Defence policy
  • Security challenges
  • Crisis management
  • Military capability development
  • Ethics and warfare
  • Gender and security
  • Peace and conflict dynamics

War Studies at the Swedish Defence University combines theoretical knowledge with practical applications relevant to contemporary security environments.

PhD Specialization: Gender, Peace and Security

The Department of War Studies is currently recruiting a doctoral candidate specializing in Gender, Peace and Security.

The position focuses on research within the field of feminist security studies and offers considerable flexibility for candidates to develop innovative and impactful research projects.

Potential research themes may include:

Gender and Military Organizations

Research may examine:

  • Gender integration in military institutions
  • Diversity and inclusion within armed forces
  • Leadership and gender dynamics
  • Gendered experiences within military structures

Gender and War Preparedness

Possible research areas include:

  • Gender dimensions of national defence preparedness
  • Civil preparedness and resilience
  • Security planning and gender inclusion
  • Community resilience during crises and conflict

Gender and Warfare

Candidates may explore:

  • Gendered impacts of war
  • Military operations and gender perspectives
  • Women’s participation in armed conflict
  • Gender and military strategy

Gender and Peace Processes

Research topics may include:

  • Women’s participation in peace negotiations
  • Gender-inclusive peacebuilding
  • Peace agreements and gender outcomes
  • Post-conflict recovery and reconciliation

Feminist Security Studies

Applicants may investigate broader questions such as:

  • Gender norms and security governance
  • Feminist approaches to defence policy
  • Gendered understandings of security
  • Critical security studies perspectives
  • The relationship between power, conflict, and gender

What the PhD Programme Includes

The doctoral programme consists of:

Four Years of Fully Funded Doctoral Study

The programme comprises:

  • 240 ECTS credits in total
  • 180 ECTS dedicated to doctoral thesis research
  • 60 ECTS of doctoral-level coursework

Research Supervision

Successful candidates will receive:

  • Dedicated academic supervision
  • Access to experienced researchers
  • Research mentoring
  • Academic development support

Teaching Opportunities

PhD candidates may also participate in:

  • Teaching activities
  • Academic administration
  • Departmental support activities

These duties may account for up to 20% of working time and can extend employment to a maximum of five years.

Financial Benefits

One of the strongest features of this opportunity is its generous funding package.

Monthly Salary

Civilian doctoral students receive:

Starting salary: SEK 32,600 per month

The salary increases throughout the doctoral programme according to the university’s salary scale.

Annual Research Funding

Doctoral candidates receive additional support for:

  • Research travel
  • Academic conferences
  • Literature purchases
  • Research-related expenses

Funding available:

Up to SEK 30,000 per year

Additional Benefits

Students also benefit from:

  • Full employment status
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Access to university resources
  • International research networks
  • Collaboration with leading scholars

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet both general and specific doctoral admission requirements.

General Requirements

Applicants must possess one of the following:

  • A Master’s degree
  • Equivalent qualifications obtained in Sweden or abroad
  • At least 240 ECTS credits, including 60 ECTS credits at postgraduate level

Specific Academic Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • At least 90 ECTS credits in social sciences or equivalent
  • At least 30 ECTS credits in:
    • War Studies
    • International Relations
    • Security Studies
    • Peace and Conflict Studies
    • Political Science
    • Closely related fields

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Excellent English writing skills
  • Excellent English speaking skills

Applications may be submitted in:

  • English
  • Swedish
  • Danish
  • Norwegian

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

Academic Excellence

Reviewers will assess:

  • Previous academic performance
  • Research achievements
  • Quality of academic writing

Research Proposal Quality

Strong proposals should demonstrate:

  • Originality
  • Theoretical awareness
  • Methodological clarity
  • Analytical depth
  • Relevance to Gender, Peace and Security

Independent Research Capacity

Candidates should show:

  • Critical thinking skills
  • Research independence
  • Ability to develop scholarly arguments

Additional Merits

The following may strengthen an application:

  • Publications
  • Research experience
  • Professional experience
  • Military education
  • Security studies expertise
  • Gender studies background

Why This PhD Opportunity Stands Out

This position offers a rare opportunity to conduct advanced research at the intersection of:

  • Gender Studies
  • Security Studies
  • Peacebuilding
  • International Relations
  • Defence Policy
  • Conflict Studies

Students will work within a highly respected academic environment while contributing research that can shape future understanding of security, defence, conflict, and peace processes.

The university strongly values diversity and welcomes applicants from different cultural, ethnic, and national backgrounds.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 24 August 2026

Click here for more and to apply

Applications must be submitted before midnight CET/CEST on the closing date.

Successful candidates are expected to begin their doctoral studies in January 2027.

For aspiring researchers interested in gender, conflict, peacebuilding, and security studies, this fully funded PhD position offers an exceptional opportunity to pursue impactful research while receiving a competitive salary and extensive research support.

Click here for more global scholarship opportunities

 

The post Fully Funded PhD in Gender, Peace and Security at the Swedish Defence University 2027 | Salary, Research Funding and International Applicants Welcome appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: OECD Affordable Housing and Homelessness Internship 2026 in Paris, France: Fully Paid Opportunity for Graduate Students
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Are you a graduate student passionate about affordable housing, social policy, homelessness prevention, and evidence-based public policy? The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is offering an exciting internship opportunity within its Directorate for Employment, Labour and Social Affairs (ELS), Social Policy Division, based in Paris, France. This highly competitive internship provides an exceptional […]

The post OECD Affordable Housing and Homelessness Internship 2026 in Paris, France: Fully Paid Opportunity for Graduate Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a graduate student passionate about affordable housing, social policy, homelessness prevention, and evidence-based public policy? The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is offering an exciting internship opportunity within its Directorate for Employment, Labour and Social Affairs (ELS), Social Policy Division, based in Paris, France.

This highly competitive internship provides an exceptional opportunity for talented graduate students to contribute to international research, policy development, and data analysis related to affordable housing and homelessness across OECD and European Union countries.

The internship is expected to begin in Fall 2026 and will run for a duration of four to six months, offering participants the chance to work alongside leading policy experts while contributing to projects that directly influence housing and social policies worldwide.

Application Deadline: 24 June 2026

About the OECD

The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is an international organisation consisting of 38 member countries dedicated to promoting policies that improve economic and social well-being around the world.

The OECD serves as a global knowledge hub, bringing together governments, policymakers, researchers, and experts to address some of the world’s most pressing social, economic, and environmental challenges.

Its work spans numerous sectors, including:

  • Economic development
  • Education and skills
  • Employment and labour markets
  • Climate change
  • Digital transformation
  • Healthcare
  • Taxation
  • Governance
  • Innovation
  • Social inclusion
  • Housing policy

Through evidence-based research and international cooperation, the OECD develops standards, policy recommendations, and comparative analyses that help countries build stronger and more inclusive societies.

About the Directorate for Employment, Labour and Social Affairs (ELS)

The Directorate for Employment, Labour and Social Affairs is the OECD’s central body for promoting inclusive growth and social well-being.

Its work focuses on:

  • Creating quality jobs
  • Reducing poverty and social exclusion
  • Strengthening human capital
  • Improving healthcare systems
  • Managing migration effectively
  • Enhancing social protection systems
  • Supporting housing inclusion initiatives

Within ELS, the Social Policy Division leads important work on affordable housing, homelessness, housing exclusion, and broader social policy challenges affecting millions of people worldwide.

OECD Affordable Housing and Homelessness Internship Overview

The Social Policy Division is seeking a motivated and talented intern to support ongoing research, analytical work, and policy development relating to:
